title: euphorbia macrorhiza
intro: Euphorbia macrorhiza, also known as the largeleaf spurge or devil's horsebane, is a species of plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that can help to reduce pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Euphorbia macrorhiza are numerous, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are typically dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
